note: What separates an episode that got a federal declaration from one that didn't — every empirical metric, ranked by separation power (AUC). No forecast. PA is obligated, IHP approved.
insight: EXTENT separates, peak intensity barely does: how many counties cross a hazard bar is the strongest single signal (AUC ~0.70), while peak EF / gust / hail sit near a coin-flip (~0.55). Two notes from the fit: rainfall RARITY (Atlas-14) separates worse than raw rain extent, and — holding extent fixed — RURAL counties cross the line more easily than metros (the per-capita threshold). Declarations are only moderately predictable from weather alone; assessment and cost-share politics carry the rest.

provenance.unit: state-storm episode — each NOAA Storm Events episode's counties aggregated to the state level
provenance.declaredRule: declared = at least one designated county of the episode fell inside a FEMA disaster (OpenFEMA, state level). ANY declaration counts — this view is declared-vs-not, not per-hazard attribution
provenance.metric: AUC = chance a random declared episode scores higher than a random non-declared one on that metric (0.5 = no separation, 1.0 = perfect). 'combined' is a small in-sample logistic fit across the extent/severity metrics — a separability read, not a forecast
provenance.envelope: each metric is the peak/sum across the episode's counties (peak envelope or footprint sum)
provenance.caveat: Storm Events under-records flood damage; river-stage and snow metrics are sparse (gaged/cold-season only). ARI via NOAA Atlas-14; exposure via Census (population only — no housing key in this environment)
provenance.source: scripts/build_context.py over data/state_panel.json (NOAA Storm Events + OpenFEMA + USGS + Atlas-14 + Census)

separators.label: counties with a notable hazard
separators.basis: how many counties in the storm system cleared a real hazard bar (over flood stage, or a damaging wind/hail/tornado)

separators.label: counties above flood stage
separators.basis: counties where a gaged river reached NWS flood stage during the episode

separators.label: rainfall footprint (rain × area)
separators.basis: episode rainfall summed across affected counties — depth and breadth together

separators.label: peak 1-day rainfall
separators.basis: heaviest single-day rainfall at any county in the system

separators.basis: NOAA Storm Events property-damage estimate, summed over the system (under-records flood)

separators.label: counties in the storm system
separators.basis: raw size of the storm system in counties

separators.label: rainfall rarity (Atlas-14)
separators.basis: how rare the event rainfall is against the county's own climatology (recurrence interval)

separators.label: max hail size
separators.basis: largest hailstone (intensity, not extent)

separators.label: population in affected counties
separators.basis: people living in the affected counties (Census)

separators.basis: strongest tornado in the system (intensity, not extent)
